description: §26.8.2 / §26.8.3 — a variant-prefixed `@apply` token (`.y { @apply hover:bg-blue-500; }`, a `:` at bracket-depth 0) fires E-APPLY-VARIANT-UNSUPPORTED (Error). Variants require a nested/companion selector and cannot be flat-inlined in v1 (a bounded follow-on, lift when variants land). Classified as VARIANT before registry resolution, so it is NOT the UNKNOWN code.
rationale: §26.8.2: `hover:bg-blue-500` carries a variant prefix (`hover:`) — a nested/companion selector, not flat inlining → E-APPLY-VARIANT-UNSUPPORTED (Error, §26.8.3), anchored at the `@apply` span. `resolveApplyToken` classifies the variant BEFORE registry resolution, so the token fires VARIANT-UNSUPPORTED, never UNKNOWN-UTILITY or NON-INLINABLE. Lift when the §26.3 variant surface lands in `@apply`.
